title: THE DRAFT
subtitle: A Quiet Evening in the Warehouse · 7:14 PM
epigraph_upright: The Magician makes. He chooses the day. He has tools that obey him. The pieces are at the edge of being something.
epigraph_reversed: Stagnation. Sealed doors. Drafts that won't finish. A workbench that lies to him about what it's for.
scene_description: The river is doing what rivers do. The bay door is shut. Three pieces in the inner cluster are within a beat of being something. The bird-watcher is already at the river window — she gestured up at the osprey nest before you started writing. Elicia is bringing a tape. John will walk over from the diner with a bag. The steamboat is on the workbench, a bare hull. You don't have to finish it tonight.
direction_hint: Make three pieces. Connect three of the people who show up. Don't finish the steamboat unless you mean to.
opening_log_lines: The cathedral hums. The river side is quiet enough you can hear the still drip.
Click [b]PHASE: ACTION[/b] anytime for the rules. Try [b]MOVE ↪[/b] to walk.
The bird-watcher already has the osprey angle. Don't startle her.

stagnation_thresholds.label: you've been at this piece a while
a draft is curling at the edges

notes: Easy-mode scaffold for the Magician. Friendly roster (Elicia / John / Nicola / Mackenzie-remote / the bird-watcher) plus one weirdo (the drifter at the bay door). The Demon does NOT appear in THE DRAFT — he's reserved for the medium + hard scenarios. Steamboat lives on the workbench but the win condition explicitly does NOT require completing it; the medium and hard scenarios re-tune around finishing-vs-not. Spec source: lore/_TAROT_GAUNTLET_SCENARIOS.md.
